catalog.rationale: Its extended red sensitivity enables infrared-like effects while remaining easier to handle than true infrared HIE.

reference.relationship: Independent interpretive emulation; researched, not measured

dossier.tagline: Deep red-filter skies, pale foliage, and moderate conventional grain.
dossier.portrait: SFX 200 is modeled around deep red-filter skies, pale foliage, and moderate conventional grain. Its extended red sensitivity enables infrared-like effects while remaining easier to handle than true infrared HIE. Grainlab treats that identity as a linked capture, emulsion, process, and output system—not as a color cast pasted over a digital file.

facts.label: CANON RANK
facts.note: Curatorial significance rank; not a sales chart

chapters.title: Why SFX 200 belongs in the century canon
chapters.lede: Its extended red sensitivity enables infrared-like effects while remaining easier to handle than true infrared HIE.

details.label: Material
details.value: B&W extended-red negative; extended red; nominal speed / EI reference 200.

details.label: Image structure
details.value: cubic crystal interpretation, mixed emulsion mapping, medium presentation-scale grain.

notes: The canon balances cultural use, technical influence, geographic/manufacturer breadth, process diversity, and surviving documentation; rank is curatorial rather than a claimed global sales measurement.

chapters.title: Tone, color, and material signature
chapters.lede: The target is deep red-filter skies, pale foliage, and moderate conventional grain.

details.label: Curve
details.value: Toe 0.16 · development gamma 1.12 · shoulder 0.25 · scan contrast 1.00.

details.label: Color / spectral posture
details.value: Monochrome conversion uses extended red weighting before the film curve.

details.label: Grain
details.value: medium metallic-silver clumps; cubic geometry; mixed architecture.

details.label: Halation
details.value: 15% thresholded scene-highlight scatter before the film curve; intentionally stock/process dependent.

notes: Grain is signal-dependent and spatially correlated across the full frame; it is not independent RGB noise or a repeating overlay.

chapters.title: From DSLR or iPhone to a plausible negative
chapters.lede: Begin from a neutral SDR rendering with restrained sharpening and noise reduction. Convert through the stated spectral weights before the silver curve; do not desaturate a contrasty RGB image and call it film.

details.label: Input normalization
details.value: Prefer RAW developed neutrally when available. For HEIC/JPEG, choose the least processed rendition; avoid baked Vivid styles, portrait relighting, clarity, and denoise.

details.label: Dynamic range
details.value: Map HDR/Display-P3 into a stable SDR working image without clipping, preserving highlight and shadow separation before the film response.

details.label: White balance
details.value: Neutralize obvious camera casts first, then let the extended red stock balance and emulsion sensitivity create the intended response.

details.label: Hard limit
details.value: Visible-RGB DSLR or iPhone input cannot reconstruct wavelengths the sensor or its IR-cut filter never recorded; this is a tonal analogy, not spectral recovery.

notes: Apple ProRAW and camera RAW still need a deliberate neutral development; RAW is not automatically scene-linear or aesthetically neutral after a converter renders it.

chapters.title: A stage-by-stage rendering target
chapters.lede: sRGB decode → +0.00 EV scene placement → 1.150/0.960/0.820 channel sensitivity → toe 0.16, γ 1.12, shoulder 0.25 → B&W extended-red negative chemistry → 15% thresholded halation → medium silver structure

details.label: Scene
details.value: Decode to linear light, apply +0.00 EV and the stock sensitivity before tone shaping.

details.label: Emulsion
details.value: Build the toe, midtone gamma, shoulder, crossover, saturation compression, and correlated image structure as one response.

details.label: Lab / optics
details.value: Model B&W extended-red negative, chemical fog, local density contrast, and highlight scatter before the output transform.

details.label: Scan / print
details.value: Use a neutral scan baseline, set black and white points after the film curve, and sharpen subject detail at final output size.

notes: All parameters are bounded artistic approximations derived from published characteristics and process knowledge, not digitized manufacturer sensitometric or spectral measurements.

bestFor: Subjects that benefit from deep red-filter skies, pale foliage, and moderate conventional grain.
Form, gesture, texture, and light where color separation can be translated into deliberate gray relationships.
watchFor: Visible-RGB DSLR or iPhone input cannot reconstruct wavelengths the sensor or its IR-cut filter never recorded; this is a tonal analogy, not spectral recovery.
Stacking a strong camera picture style, computational HDR, or sharpening underneath the emulation.
Judging grain at 200% instead of at 100% and intended output size.
fieldNotes: Expose for the material idea: deep red-filter skies, pale foliage, and moderate conventional grain.
Try the profile on neutral daylight, mixed light, skin, foliage, and a flat wall before deciding what its name means.
dossier.disclaimer: SFX 200 is an independent Grainlab artistic emulation informed by cited public literature and historical material. It is not endorsed by Ilford, is not a measured ICC/LUT profile, and cannot reproduce a specific batch, lens, exposure, chemistry, printer, scanner, paper, aging state, or wavelengths absent from the digital source.
